data.title: Crypto Market Maker Portofino Technologies CFO Mark Blackborough Left the Business
data.description:
• Mark Blackborough, the chief financial officer of Portofino Technologies, has left the business after just joining last September.
  • The crypto trading firm recently told CoinDesk that it was considering opening offices in both New York and Singapore.

data.title: Bitcoin Tumbles Below $80K Alongside 5% Plunge in Nasdaq as China Tariff Tiff Escalates
data.description:
• Bitcoin and tech stocks tumbled Thursday as investors thought twice about yesterday's historic rally.
  • A late-morning White House clarification raised the effective China tariff rate to 145% from 125%, helping push the Nasdaq from a 4% loss to 5.5%; bitcoin followed suit, sliding back to $79,000.
  • Gold surged to a record high and the dollar sank, as traders shifted focus from inflation to geopolitical risk.

data.title: Centrifuge Taps Wormhole to Launch Multichain Tokenization Platform
data.description:
• Centrifuge V3 will let users manage tokenized assets across chains from a single interface.
  • The system is launching with a $230 million tokenized U.S. Treasury fund managed by Anemoy.
  • Wormhole will provide multichain infrastructure to support liquidity and composability.

data.title: TRX Rallies 10% as Tether Mints $1B on Tron Amid Global Trade Tensions
data.description:
• TRX rebounds 10% in 3 days, defying market jitters with a strong double-bottom and bullish momentum.
  • Tether mints 1B USDT on Tron, signaling institutional confidence amid global economic uncertainty.

data.title: Cardano’s Hoskinson Sees Bitcoin Touching $250K, Tech Giants Adopting Stablecoins
data.description:
• Bitcoin could reach $250,000 by the end of this year or next, driven by tech giants entering the crypto space, according to Cardano founder Charles Hoskinson.
  • Despite recent market turbulence, Hoskinson said he remains optimistic about bitcoin's future, citing potential stabilization and increased adoption of cryptocurrencies.
  • Forthcoming U.S. legislation and geopolitical shifts are expected to bolster the crypto market, with stablecoins possibly seeing widespread adoption by major technology companies.

data.title: BNB Plunges 11.6% Before Staging Massive Recovery Amid Trump Tariff Concerns
data.description:
• BNB experienced dramatic price swings, plummeting 11.6% from $589.78 to $521.16 before recovering to $585.61, demonstrating exceptional market volatility.
  • President Trump's rapid implementation of wide-ranging import tariffs has significantly impacted cryptocurrency markets, with Bitcoin's correlation to traditional assets shifting dramatically.
  • VanEck has initiated the process for a BNB ETF in the U.S., sparking renewed investor interest despite ongoing regulatory challenges for Binance.

data.title: Dogecoin ETF Race Joined by 21Shares
data.description:
• 21Shares has filed to introduce a spot dogecoin (DOGE) ETF in the U.S., joining Bitwise and Grayscale.
  • The asset manager has partnered with the House of Doge — the Dogecoin Foundation’s corporate arm — to help promote the fund.

data.title: Dogecoin Surges 21% Amid Crypto Comeback, Holds Key Support at $0.142
data.description:
• DOGE rebounds from a sharp 21.2% plunge, establishing support at $0.142-$0.145 with significant accumulation volumes during recovery.
  • Geopolitical tensions and trade disputes create market volatility, with DOGE showing resilience compared to broader crypto market declines.
  • Technical indicators point to potential bullish reversal patterns forming across multiple timeframes, with key resistance at $0.16-$0.168.

data.title: Wrapped BTC Holders Can Now Secure 6% APY on Base via Umoja
data.description:
• Investors can deposit cbBTC to Umoja and earn an annual yield of 6%.
  • Umoja achieves the yield by deploying deposited capital on a range of trading strategies like covered calls and abritrage.
  • Japanese firm recently started to earn a yield on its bitcoin holdings by purchasing put options and selling the premium as price drops.

data.title: U.S. CPI Declined in March; Core Rate Rose Just 0.1%.
data.description:
• U.S. CPI rose far less than forecasts in March.
  • The price of bitcoin rose modestly following the data.
  • The Fed next meets in May, and traders for the moment are expecting the central bank to stay on hold.
